title: Singapore cops seize $42 million mansion, freeze $772k bank account of suspected Nvidia AI GPU smugglers individuals alleged to have illegally exported data center servers to China charged with fraud, money laundering
summary: Singapore authorities seize assets of four individuals suspected of smuggling Nvidia AI GPUs. They allegedly used Singapore as a transshipment point to export data center servers to China illegally. The individuals face charges of fraud and money laundering.
insight: Singapore's lax export controls are exploited by global smuggling rings.

title: Possible AMD RX 7900 XTX engineering sample with red PCB surfaces prototype came with no backplate & custom VBIOS but matches RX 7900 GRE specs
summary: A potential AMD RX 7900 XTX engineering sample has been spotted online, featuring a custom VBIOS and a red PCB with no backplate. The prototype seems to match the specs of the RX 7900 GRE, a binned-down model of the RX 7900 XTX. This find offers a rare glimpse into the engineering process of high-end graphics cards.
insight: Insights into AMD's engineering process and binned GPU models revealed

title: Apple's Hide My Email service reportedly reveals users' actual email addresses with little effort Cupertino has seemingly known about the problem for a year but has yet to fix it
summary: Apple's Hide My Email service still exposes users' actual email addresses despite known issues.
insight: Users' private info remains vulnerable despite Apple's awareness of the problem.

title: Intel expands production of photomasks in California: EUV and High-NA EUV in the focal point
summary: Intel is expanding its Bowers Campus in Santa Clara to produce more photomasks in-house, a crucial move as process technologies become increasingly complex. This move will allow Intel to have greater control and efficiency in its manufacturing process. The expansion will focus on EUV and High-NA EUV photomasks.
insight: Intel gains control over photomask production to advance manufacturing efficiency

title: Google testing controversial webcam-based reCAPTCHA that asks for a hand scan to prove you're human testers beat it with a stock photo
summary: Google is testing a reCAPTCHA feature that uses webcam to verify user identity by scanning hand movements, but testers have already found a way to bypass it using a stock photo.
insight: Security measures can be easily exploited with creative workarounds.

title: Elon Musk categorically denies SpaceX is making an AI device with proprietary OS says rumors of a handheld thinner than an iPhone are 'utterly false'
summary: Elon Musk denies reports of a handheld AI device from SpaceX, calling rumors 'utterly false'. The alleged device reportedly features a proprietary OS and advanced AI capabilities. SpaceX has not confirmed the existence of such a project.
insight: SpaceX's AI ambitions may be shrouded in secrecy, sparking speculation

title: Xbox reportedly testing a way to digitize physical games in the wake of PlayStation killing game discs feature said to go back to Xbox One-era games
summary: Microsoft is reportedly testing a feature to digitize physical Xbox games going back to the Xbox One era, allowing owners to access digital copies tied to their physical disc.
insight: Enhancing backwards compatibility and preserving gaming library ownership

title: Tesla hires 17-year Intel veteran responsible for billion-dollar fab startups Gary Jiang likely chosen to oversee fab efforts for Terafab's licensing of 14A
summary: Tesla hires Intel veteran Gary Jiang to lead fab efforts, likely overseeing 14A licensing for Terafab.
insight: Strategic fab leadership to boost Tesla's 14A chip production capabilities.

title: Sony officially kills the PlayStation disc, ending physical game production in 2028 shutting down the PlayStation Store on the PlayStation 3 and PS Vita systems
summary: Sony has announced the end of physical game production and the PlayStation Store for PS3 and PS Vita systems. This marks a significant shift towards digital game distribution. The PlayStation disc is being phased out in 2028.
insight: Physical media decline paves way for digital gaming dominance

title: SONOFF NSPanel Pro Gen2 touch control panel review with eWeLink and Home Assistant
summary: SONOFF NSPanel Pro Gen2 review covers its unboxing, teardown, and setup process with eWeLink and Home Assistant, showcasing its smart home control features and compatibility with various devices.
insight: Advanced smart home control for seamless automation and connectivity

title: PaperBoy Game Boy Emulator works at 60 FPS on ESP32-S3 E Ink devkit
summary: A Game Boy emulator running at 60 FPS on an ESP32-S3 E Ink devkit has been achieved by Wenting Zhang's PaperBoy project, utilizing a 4.7-inch display with a raw row/column driver interface to bypass the normal waveform method. The emulator leverages the device's unique display capabilities to deliver high refresh rates. This project showcases the potential for high-performance gaming on low-power, E Ink displays.
insight: Bypassing waveform method enables high refresh rates on E Ink displays

title: Sipeed NanoKVM-Go A 4K USB-C KVM with Recall-like function, AI integration (Crowdfunding)
summary: Sipeed NanoKVM-Go is a 4K USB-C KVM with AI integration, offering remote control and AI-powered features.
insight: Streamlined remote access with AI-powered features for increased productivity.

title: Infineon wraps up $650M ams OSRAM sensors buy
summary: Infineon has completed its $650 million acquisition of ams OSRAM sensors. This move is expected to support Infineon's shift towards more application-ready designs. The ams OSRAM components will help Infineon expand its product offerings.
insight: Enhanced sensor capabilities drive application-ready design solutions.

title: Video: The day Edge AI got 'more real' with sensors
summary: Panelists at Sensors Converge discussed their favorite edge AI use cases, showcasing advancements in sensor technology and artificial intelligence integration.
insight: Edge AI enhances real-world applications with real-time, localized data processing.

title: Apple's Hide My Email feature may be exposing users' real addresses
summary: Apple's Hide My Email feature may be exposing users' real addresses due to a potential security flaw. The feature, available through iCloud+, creates random email addresses that forward messages to the user's real inbox. However, this process may not be secure.
insight: Random email forwarding may inadvertently reveal users' real email addresses

title: Testing AMD Radeon's Biggest-Ever Software Upgrade: FSR 4.1 on RDNA 3
summary: AMD's FSR 4.1 INT8 upscaler enhances image quality on RDNA 3 GPUs, offering a significant upgrade. This upgrade is comparable to the RDNA 4 version. We compare the visual quality, performance, and match of this RDNA 3 upgrade.
insight: RDNA 3 GPUs see substantial image quality upgrades with FSR 4.1.

title: Amazon says restricting Fire TV sideloading is about safety. It's also about piracy
summary: Amazon restricts sideloading on Fire TV due to malware and piracy concerns.
insight: Piracy prevention drives Amazon's sideloading restriction on newer Fire TV devices

title: OCCT 17 introduces a redesigned modular Memory Test
summary: OCCT 17 introduces a redesigned modular Memory Test with improved performance.
insight: Enhanced memory testing for better PC performance and reliability

title: Former PlayStation exec says PC ports were about marketing, not money and Sony's new leadership disagrees
summary: Shawn Layden claims PlayStation games on PC were a marketing strategy, not for revenue. This contradicts Sony's new leadership, who may have different views on the decision.
insight: Marketing prioritization over revenue generation in PC ports

title: Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 specs surface, Z Flip discontinuation rumors, Galaxy rollables may arrive in 2028
summary: The Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 series may feature significant design changes, with the regular Z Fold 8 receiving a wider and shorter footprint to compete with the rumored iPhone Fold. The Z Fold 8 Ultra is expected to be the flagship foldable device this year. Samsung may also introduce Galaxy rollables in 2028.
insight: Samsung's foldable innovation drives competition with Apple iPhone Fold.

title: Google launches Nano Banana 2 Lite for fast, low-cost AI image generation
summary: Google's Nano Banana 2 Lite model generates AI images rapidly at low cost.
insight: Cost-effective AI image generation for high-volume workloads is now accessible.

title: Sony will stop making physical PlayStation games starting in 2028
summary: Sony will stop producing physical PlayStation games starting in 2028, shifting focus to digital formats.
insight: Shift to digital-only games impacts physical game collection enthusiasts.

title: WinRAR 7.23 fixes bugs, jokes that users are finally paying for it
summary: WinRAR 7.23 fixes two security vulnerabilities and updates its extraction library.
insight: Vital security patches make this update worth installing immediately always.

title: Steam Machine benchmarks show single RAM stick setup can cost up to 20% performance
summary: Steam Machines ship with a single 16GB DDR5 SO-DIMM, limiting performance. This forces the system into single-channel mode, not dual-channel. This can result in a 20% performance loss.
insight: Single RAM stick limits Steam Machine's performance capabilities by 20%.

title: Cinder City becomes the first PC game to recommend 64GB of RAM, and at the worst time
summary: Cinder City is the first PC game to recommend 64GB of RAM, diverging from the trend of demanding high-end GPUs for optimal performance. Its requirement for a relatively modest RTX 4060 (8GB) GPU is unusual, given the increasing emphasis on powerful graphics cards. This shift in system requirements may signal a change in game development priorities.
insight: Games now prioritize memory over graphics processing power requirements

title: AMD Zen 6 leak reveals third core type, corroborating rumored Sony handheld SoC codenamed Canis
summary: A leak suggests AMD's Zen 6 CPU lineup may introduce a new 'Low Power core type', akin to Intel's low-power efficiency cores.
insight: AMD Zen 6 could rival Intel's CPU efficiency with new core type.

title: Apple's fight with Epic over App Store fees reaches the Supreme Court
summary: Apple's appeal against contempt ruling in Epic Games App Store dispute heads to the Supreme Court.
insight: Antitrust implications for tech giants' business practices are at stake here.

title: Anthropic's Fable 5 shutdown ends with tougher safeguards and federal approval
summary: Anthropic's Fable model has undergone shutdown and restructuring to improve safeguards, receiving federal approval.
insight: Model's vulnerabilities expose need for stronger guardrails and oversight.
